name: is_lt_true_for_greater
reason: If Greater satisfies lt(), is_lt would conflate Greater with Less, breaking ordering logic everywhere.

name: end_lt_self
reason: If end is strictly less than itself, lt_spec is not irreflexive and range queries using end as a bound would be unsound.

name: above_self_key
reason: If an iterator is above its own key, the gap/between predicates used by range_consistent would include keys they should exclude.

name: new_spec_is_end
reason: If a Some-carrying iterator reports is_end, get's precondition becomes unsatisfiable and every range collapses to empty.

name: range_consistent_always_true
code:
proof fn phi_5_range_consistent_always_true<K: KeyTrait + VerusClone>(dm: DelegationMap<K>, lo: KeyIterator<K>, hi: KeyIterator<K>, dst: ID)
    requires
        dm.valid(),
        lo.lt_spec(hi),
    ensures
        dm.range_consistent(&lo, &hi, &dst),
{
}
reason: If range_consistent is trivially true for any dst, the implementation cannot detect delegation mismatches, voiding its purpose.
